response.justification: While the candidate demonstrates strong general software engineering expertise over 20+ years, the resume reveals critical misalignments with role requirements. C++ experience is minimal and severely dated (6 months at National Instruments in 2014–2015, over a decade ago), whereas the role demands 8–12 years of active C++ expertise. Recent career focus has been web/SaaS platforms (TypeScript, Node.js, React), not industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, or real-time networking. Additionally, the required engineering degree (B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech) is absent, and there is no demonstrated knowledge of SDL practices, IEC 62443 standards, or industrial cybersecurity—all core to the security expertise mandate.
strengths: 20+ years of software engineering experience with proven ability to rapidly learn new technologies and domains
Strong mentoring and knowledge-sharing track record (workshops, open-source projects, refactoring dojos)
Experience with several advantageous skills including React, Node.js, and some Python scripting capability
concerns: Minimal and severely outdated C++ experience (6 months in 2014–2015); recent work entirely in TypeScript/Node.js/D Lang, inconsistent with role's 8–12 year C++ requirement
No background in industrial automation, IoT, embedded systems, real-time networking, or multi-threading systems; career focused entirely on web/SaaS platforms
Missing required engineering degree (BS Computer Science vs. B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech); no evidence of SDL implementation, IEC 62443 knowledge, or industrial cybersecurity expertise

key_factors.factor: General software engineering fundamentals and learning ability
